summary:
This joint resolution’s primary purpose is to express congressional disapproval of the District of Columbia Council’s approval of the Comprehensive Policing and Justice Reform Amendment Act of 2022.

The resolution identifies the District measure as D.C. Act 24-781, enacted by the Council of the District of Columbia on January 19, 2023. It states that the act was transmitted to Congress on January 26, 2023, under section 602(c)(1) of the District of Columbia Home Rule Act, which provides for congressional review of District legislation.

The resolution contains one operative provision: Congress disapproves of the District Council action approving that act. It does not describe or amend the substantive provisions of the District law, and it does not make separate changes to federal law beyond the stated disapproval. The text includes no exceptions, conditions, or delayed effective date. It notes that the resolution passed the House of Representatives on April 19, 2023, and was received in the Senate and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs on April 20, 2023.
